meta.name: Caribbean Oxtail Stew
meta.subtitle: Jamaican-style oxtail simmered until falling-off-the-bone in a rich brown gravy with butter beans

source.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
source.attribution: Modeled from common Jamaican oxtail patterns: browned oxtails, aromatic gravy, thyme + scallion, optional scotch bonnet, finished with butter beans.

yield.notes: Rich and filling; commonly served with rice and peas or plain rice.

ingredients.label: Oxtail pieces
ingredients.notes: Trim excess surface fat if desired; keep some for flavor.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Common Caribbean practice for seasoning/washing meat.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust later depending on stock and soy sauce use.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Adds depth and color; common in many homes.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances the savory gravy; can reduce if you prefer less sweet.

ingredients.label: Browning sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional. Use sparingly; it deepens the classic brown stew look.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce (or tamari)
ingredients.notes: Optional but common for deep savory flavor. Use tamari for GF.

ingredients.label: Beef stock or water
ingredients.notes: Start with 5 cups; add as needed to keep oxtails barely covered.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet (whole)
ingredients.notes: Keep whole for aroma; do NOT burst unless you want it hotter.

ingredients.label: Butter beans (cooked/drained)
ingredients.notes: Add near the end so they don't break down.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch + water slurry
ingredients.notes: Optional: 1 tbsp cornstarch + 2 tbsp water, to thicken gravy if needed.

ingredients.label: White vinegar or apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Optional: brightens and balances the stew at the end.

steps.title: Season the oxtails
instructions: Toss with lime juice, salt, black pepper, allspice, paprika (optional), half the garlic, half the ginger (optional), and half the scallion.
Rest 20–30 minutes (or refrigerate up to overnight for deeper flavor).

steps.title: Brown the oxtails
instructions: Heat a heavy pot over medium-high; add oil.
Brown oxtails in batches on multiple sides (6–10 minutes total per batch) to build deep flavor.
Remove browned oxtails to a plate; reduce heat to medium.

steps.title: Build the stew base
instructions: In the same pot, add onion (and optional bell pepper/carrot). Saute 4–5 minutes until softened.
Stir in brown sugar and cook 20–30 seconds to lightly caramelize (watch closely).
Stir in tomato paste (optional) for 30 seconds.
Add stock/water, soy sauce (optional), browning sauce (optional), thyme, bay leaves (optional), and whole scotch bonnet (optional).
Scrape the bottom to release browned bits, then return oxtails to the pot.

steps.title: Braise until tender
instructions: Bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook 2 to 3 hours until oxtails are tender (check at 2 hours).
Stir occasionally and add a little water/stock if the liquid drops below the meat.
Skim excess fat if desired for a cleaner gravy.

steps.title: Add butter beans and finish the gravy
instructions: When oxtails are tender, add butter beans and simmer uncovered 8–12 minutes to warm through and slightly reduce the sauce.
If you want a thicker gravy, stir in a cornstarch slurry a little at a time and simmer 1–2 minutes until glossy.
Taste and adjust salt/pepper. Optional: add a tiny splash of vinegar to brighten.
Remove thyme stems, bay leaves, and whole scotch bonnet before serving.

adapt.notes: Brown oxtails first. Pressure cook with aromatics + liquid 45–55 min (depending on size), natural release 10–15 min, then reduce uncovered and add butter beans at the end.
Skip scotch bonnet or keep it whole and remove early. Avoid bursting pepper.
Reduce brown sugar to 1 tsp, and rely on browned onions + tomato paste for balance.

fridge.notes: Oxtail tastes even better next day. Cool quickly; reheat to steaming hot.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in fridge.

reheat.notes: Add a splash of water/stock to loosen gravy when reheating.

batchPlanHints.notes: Excellent for batch cooking and freezer portions.
